claim: Infrastructure for per-gene pretrained-model feature caching. Provides FeatureRegistry (load/join cached features from parquet, SHA256-validated), CachedFeatureManifest (write/verify cache integrity), and BatchedFeatureExtractor (abstract base for GPU-batched pretrained-model inference). No pretrained model weights are loaded in this fact — it documents the cache API that future facts (ESM-2, AlphaFold, MACE-OFF embeddings) will populate.

api_summary.FeatureRegistry: declare sources, check cached state, load one or join many as a single DataFrame indexed by locus_tag
api_summary.CachedFeatureManifest: sources dict keyed by source name, each with sha256 / version / rows / created_at; verify() recomputes SHA against the stored hash
api_summary.BatchedFeatureExtractor: abstract base class; subclasses implement extract(inputs, config) and inherit write_cache() which writes the parquet + updates the manifest

dependencies_added: pyarrow (new) — required for pandas.DataFrame.to_parquet / read_parquet; added to cell_sim/requirements.txt with a one-line rationale comment. pandas was already present.

source_detail: Session 13 scaffold, commit range documented in PROJECT_STATUS.md. No external citation; this is a repo-internal API.

context.commit_range: TBD — will be filled into PROJECT_STATUS after the final push.

caveats: This fact documents the API only. No pretrained model has been integrated yet. Subsequent sessions will add ESM-2, AlphaFold-DB lookup, and enable MACE-OFF; each will write its own measured fact (e.g. facts/parameters/esm2_650M_feature_table.json) once the parquet is populated and the downstream detector sweep has been re-measured.

Parquet IO requires pyarrow; this session installed it and added it to cell_sim/requirements.txt. The tests would fail on an environment without pyarrow — not because any feature-cache code imports pyarrow directly, but because pandas.read_parquet / to_parquet silently delegates to it.

batched_inference.BatchedFeatureExtractor is an abstract base only; no torch / transformers / mace / e3nn / biopython / rdkit / huggingface_hub imports at module load. A subclass in a future session will import its own heavy deps inside extract() — the same lazy pattern cell_sim/layer1_atomic/engine.py::MACEBackend already uses.
This session produced no MCC measurement. It is pure plumbing. The claim in this fact is about the API, not about Breuer 2019 accuracy.
